title: Prioritize live GitHub status

tasks.title: Prioritize current GitHub dashboard status and bound deployment status fan-out

documents.proposal:
# Prioritize live GitHub status

## Problem

Dashboard GitHub reads time out because deployment status enrichment performs many sequential status calls. User clarified the most important GitHub data is current/in-progress merges, builds, and deployments — not exhaustive deployment history.

## Scope

- Optimize dashboard GitHub reads for current status: open PRs, recent workflow runs, and latest/current deployments.
- Bound or parallelize deployment status lookups so `/api/state` can return within the dashboard budget.
- Preserve degraded-source behavior and read-only/local-only constraints.
- Keep enough deployment status data to show active/failing current deployment signals.

## Acceptance

- GitHub read path no longer performs 30 sequential deployment status requests for routine dashboard refresh.
- Open PR and workflow-run data remain present.
- Deployment status enrichment is bounded and focused on current/latest deployments.
- Dashboard tests pass.
- Live `/api/state` no longer reports `GITHUB_READ_TIMEOUT` under normal GitHub response timings measured in this session.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Make GitHub enrichment fit the dashboard source budget by prioritizing current status.
2. Preserve visibility into in-progress merges, builds, and deployments.
3. Avoid exhaustive sequential deployment-history status calls.
4. Keep dashboard local-only, read-only, and degraded-source safe.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given the GitHub reader fetches repository data, when deployments are present, then deployment status requests are bounded to a small current/latest subset and are not issued sequentially for all 30 deployments.
2. Given open PRs exist, when `/api/state` renders, then PR data is still available for merge-status lanes.
3. Given workflow runs exist, when `/api/state` renders, then recent build data is still available for build-status lanes.
4. Given current/latest deployments exist, when `/api/state` renders, then deployment status data is available for those bounded deployments.
5. Given live dashboard `/api/state` is checked after service restart, then it returns HTTP 200 within 8 seconds and does not show `GITHUB_READ_TIMEOUT` for normal measured GitHub response timings.

## Constraints

- Read-only; no mutation controls.
- No secrets/raw state links.
- Do not reintroduce ADV workflow query fan-out.
- Prefer current status over exhaustive historical data.

## Sign-Off

User stated: `status of in progress merges, builds, and deployments is most important`.
documents.design:
# Design

Change the GitHub dashboard reader from exhaustive sequential deployment-status enrichment to current-status-first enrichment.

Plan:
- Keep pulls and workflow runs as first-class reads.
- Fetch deployments, but only request statuses for a bounded latest subset.
- Run bounded deployment-status requests concurrently so current deployment signals fit within the dashboard source timeout.
- Keep existing degraded-source behavior for failures/timeouts.
- Add tests proving deployment status fan-out is bounded/concurrent enough and PR/build data remains included.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Updated dashboard GitHub enrichment to prioritize current status for in-progress merges, builds, and deployments. The prior reader fetched deployment statuses for up to 30 deployments sequentially, which measured around 12.8s per repo and caused `GITHUB_READ_TIMEOUT` under the 5s source budget.

Implemented fix:
- Fetch top-level GitHub endpoints (`pulls`, `workflow_runs`, `deployments`) concurrently.
- Keep open PR and workflow-run data intact.
- Bound deployment status enrichment to `DEFAULT_DEPLOYMENT_STATUS_LIMIT = 6` latest deployments.
- Fetch bounded deployment statuses concurrently.

Verification:
- `bun test bin/lib/dashboard/github.test.ts` — 7 pass.
- `bun test bin/lib/dashboard bin/lib/live-status.test.ts` — 71 pass.
- Trunk fast-forwarded to `d9cd4aeec09253b4f3d357002366c7aada49ce2b` and dashboard service restarted.
- Live `/api/state` returned HTTP 200 within 8s with no GitHub degraded sources for `pokeedge` or `pokeedge-web`.
- Detail probes for both projects returned HTTP 200.

Live URL: http://127.0.0.1:8765/

# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-26T21:24:50.000Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Given the GitHub reader fetches repository data, when deployments are present, then deployment status requests are bounded to a small current/latest subset and are not issued sequentially for all 30 deployments. | pass | github.test.ts asserts 30 deployments produce only DEFAULT_DEPLOYMENT_STATUS_LIMIT=6 status calls, not 30 sequential calls. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Given open PRs exist, when `/api/state` renders, then PR data is still available for merge-status lanes. | pass | github.test.ts asserts pulls data remains present: [{ number: 7 }]. Live /api/state returned merge/PR lanes without GitHub degradation. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Given workflow runs exist, when `/api/state` renders, then recent build data is still available for build-status lanes. | pass | github.test.ts asserts workflow_runs data remains present: [{ id: 8, status: "in_progress" }]. Dashboard suite passed. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Given current/latest deployments exist, when `/api/state` renders, then deployment status data is available for those bounded deployments. | pass | github.test.ts asserts bounded deployment_statuses for latest deployment IDs; concurrency test asserts max in-flight statuses > 1. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Given live dashboard `/api/state` is checked after service restart, then it returns HTTP 200 within 8 seconds and does not show `GITHUB_READ_TIMEOUT` for normal measured GitHub response timings. | pass | Live verification after restart: /api/state 200 within 8s; pokeedge degraded=[]; pokeedge-web degraded=[]; detail probes 200. |
| C1 | constraint | Read-only; no mutation controls. | respected | Only GitHub read implementation/tests changed; no mutation controls. |
| C2 | constraint | No secrets/raw state links. | respected | Existing GitHub token sanitization tests still pass; no raw state links added. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not reintroduce ADV workflow query fan-out. | respected | No ADV reader changes; dashboard suite still passes worker-free tests. |
| C4 | constraint | Prefer current status over exhaustive historical data. | respected | Implementation caps latest deployment statuses and prioritizes current endpoint data; exhaustive status fan-out removed. |
